title: Phase 1 Review & Integration

learning_objectives.topic: Integrate knowledge from Days 01-11 about governing equations, finite volume method, two-phase flow, phase change, and OpenFOAM programming
learning_objectives.importance: Phase 1 covers all fundamentals needed for evaporator CFD engine development; Day 12 provides opportunity to connect all concepts into a complete picture

learning_objectives.topic: Comprehensive review and integration test covering both theory and implementation
Transition from Phase 1 (Fundamentals) to Phase 2 (Geometry & Meshing) with understanding of connection between equations, discretization, and geometric representation
learning_objectives.challenge: Connecting mathematical formulations to geometric implementation in OpenFOAM mesh classes and boundary conditions

theory_sections.title: Phase 1 Knowledge Synthesis: From Equations to Implementation

equations.explanation: Complete equation set for evaporator simulation: velocity divergence with phase change, momentum, VOF transport with compression, and energy with latent heat

equations.name: Finite Volume Discretization Framework
equations.latex: \int_V \frac{\partial \phi}{\partial t} dV + \oint_{\partial V} \mathbf{F} \cdot d\mathbf{S} = \int_V S_\phi dV \quad \Rightarrow \quad a_P \phi_P + \sum_N a_N \phi_N = b_P
equations.explanation: FVM discretization framework used for all equations: time derivative, flux integration, and source terms

equations.name: Pressure-Velocity Coupling (SIMPLE/PISO)
equations.explanation: Algorithm for coupled flow with phase change source term S_expansion

equations.name: Project Success Metric
equations.explanation: Metric for evaluating integration project combining physics correctness, numerical stability, performance, and development time

equations.name: Geometry-Discretization Relationship
equations.explanation: Relationship between mesh geometric quality and discretization error: poor quality mesh increases numerical error even with high-order schemes

equations.name: SIMPLE vs PISO vs PIMPLE Selection Criteria
equations.explanation: Selection criteria for pressure-velocity coupling algorithms based on problem characteristics

openfoam_analysis.purpose: Dictionary storing solver settings, algorithms, and relaxation factors for the entire system

key_members.name: solvers
key_members.purpose: Settings for linear solvers (p, U, alpha, T)

openfoam_analysis.purpose: Dictionary specifying discretization schemes for temporal, divergence, gradient, and laplacian terms
Manages time loop, time step selection, and I/O operations

key_methods.name: setDeltaT()
key_methods.purpose: Adjust time step according to stability criteria

sections.title: Boundary Condition Integration
subsections: Inlet BCs: fixed velocity, fixed alpha (liquid), fixed temperature
Outlet BCs: zero gradient, pressure outlet
Wall BCs: no-slip, fixed T or fixed heat flux

sections.title: Turbulence Model Selection
subsections: Mixing Length model for evaporator flows

sections.title: Solver Configuration Integration
subsections: fvSolution: solver settings, relaxation, PISO parameters

sections.title: Complete Solver Workflow
subsections: 2. Initialize fields (p, U, alpha, T, properties)
5. Solve pressure equation WITH phase change source
7. Solve alpha equation WITH compression and Lee model
8. Solve energy equation WITH latent heat source
9. Update mixture properties (ρ, μ, c_p, k)

verified_facts: Expansion term: nabla dot U = m_dot (1/rho_v - 1/rho_l) - Derived from Day 11
Pressure equation with source: div(1/A grad p') = div U* - S_expansion - From Day 09 with Day 11 modification
VOF equation: ∂α/∂t + div(αU) + div[α(1-α)U_c] = ṁ/ρ_l - From Days 10 and 11
Energy with latent heat: ρc_p∂T/∂t + ρc_pU·∇T = div(k∇T) + ṁh_lv - From Day 11
fvSolution dictionary: Stores solver settings (controlDict) - OpenFOAM infrastructure
fvSchemes dictionary: Stores discretization schemes - Required for accurate solution
Time class: Manages time loop (src/OpenFOAM/db/Time/Time.H) - Critical for transient simulations
SIMPLE: For steady-state, single corrector per iteration
PISO: For transient, multiple correctors per time step
PIMPLE: For transient with large density variations
